summary: Turnaround time more than doubled from FY22 to FY23 and kept climbing into FY24 because NYCHA's aging buildings often need extensive carpentry, plastering, painting, and lead-paint abatement before a vacant unit can be re-rented, creating a large backlog that was reported on by news outlets and NYCHA's own federal monitor. Turnaround has since improved as NYCHA used dedicated City funding to clear the backlog and returned units previously held for building-wide modernization projects to the rent roll.

sources.label: NYCHA, Fiscal 2023 and Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Reports
Gothamist, "It's taking NYC over a year to fix up vacant public housing units, report shows"

oneLiner: increasing overall, though improving recently as NYCHA cleared a repair backlog
